title: Defer browse-only Date allocation into the browse branch in films search
detail: Lines 83-84 (`const startDate = new Date()` / `const endDate = addDays(startDate, SCREENING_WINDOW_DAYS)`) are consumed ONLY at line 100 inside the `browse && !query` branch. Verified: keyword/SQL-interval paths use `sql.raw(String(SCREENING_WINDOW_DAYS))` (lines 204/238) and never read these vars. Move the two declarations into the top of the `if (browse && !query)` block. Marginal perf; control-flow clarity. Medium because it's a query-handler edit.

title: Merge two passes over screeningResults into one in festival detail
detail: `response.screenings` is built via `.map()` (lines 250-257), then a separate `for` loop (lines 263-270) re-traverses screeningResults to accumulate `sections` (Set) and `upcomingCount`. Collapse into the single map body (add to Set + increment counter while mapping). Verified output bindings unchanged (lines 273-277). Medium: reorders meta accumulation; reviewer must confirm Set/counter semantics carry over exactly.

title: Hoist a Map for existing-status lookup in sync upsert loop
detail: The upsert loop does `serverStatuses.find((s) => s.filmId === status.filmId)` at line 196 — O(statusesToUpsert * serverStatuses), serverStatuses capped at 5000. Build `const serverByFilmId = new Map(serverStatuses.map((s) => [s.filmId, s]));` before the loop (serverStatuses otherwise only read at lines 154/196) and use `.get(...)`. filmId unique per user so the chosen row and insert-vs-update decision are byte-identical. Medium: inside a DB-write decision loop.

title: Extract shared festival column projection in festivals list query
detail: Authed and anon branches (lines 107-167) build two db.select projections identical for 14 of 16 columns; only isFollowing/interestLevel differ. Hoist a `const baseColumns = {...}` and spread into both selects with the differing fields appended. Same emitted SQL and result shape. Prevents the drift bug where a column added to one branch silently changes the anon payload. Medium: touches query construction.

title: Hoist likelyNeedsClassification regex array to module scope
detail: likelyNeedsClassification() rebuilds a 22-element RegExp array (lines 242-265) on every call — invoked once per screening (screening-classification.ts:60) and once per film (classify-events.ts:35). Lift to module-level `const LIKELY_NEEDS_CLASSIFICATION_PATTERNS = [...]` next to the existing hoisted EVENT_TYPE_RULES/FORMAT_RULES. All patterns stateless (.test(), no /g). Medium: classification/extraction module.

title: Hoist quickClassify/isLikelyCleanFilmTitle regex arrays to module scope
detail: quickClassify() rebuilds nonFilmPatterns (13, lines 38-52), liveBroadcastPatterns (11, lines 67-79), concertPatterns (3, lines 101-105); isLikelyCleanFilmTitle() rebuilds eventPatterns (~30, lines 149-183) — ~57 RegExp allocs per classified title across posters/service.ts and fix-placeholders.ts callers. Lift each to module-level const preserving exact entries/order. All stateless (.test(), no /g). Medium: content-classification module. Ship in the SAME PR as rm-unused-clearclassificationcache (same file); combined PR risk = medium.

title: Hoist per-country RegExp construction out of extractCountry()
detail: extractCountry() (lines 148-156) builds `new RegExp(\b${country}\b, "i")` for every entry in the 60+ COUNTRIES array on every call (~60 recompiles per parseFilmMetadata). Precompile once at module load into a parallel `{country, regex}[]` and iterate inside extractCountry, returning the same country on first .test() hit. Same patterns/boundaries/flag/first-match-wins. Pure constant hoist (allowed by scraper rule). Medium: extraction-adjacent.

title: Remove redundant getBrowser() call in CurzonScraper.initialize()
detail: Line 530 `await getBrowser();` immediately precedes line 531 `this.page = await createPage();`. Verified createPage() (browser.ts:69) calls getBrowser() internally as its first statement, and getBrowser() is an idempotent singleton. Delete line 530; this leaves getBrowser unused in the line-23 import, so drop it (`import { closeBrowser, createPage } from "../utils/browser";`). Browser still created exactly once via createPage. Medium: touches browser lifecycle.

title: Hoist static country-list array out of GardenCinemaScraper.isCountry()
detail: isCountry() (lines 184-193) allocates a fresh 32-element `countries` array per call — called once per film card from parseStats() (line 174) inside the filmCards.each loop (lines 72-129). Hoist to module-level `const COUNTRIES: readonly string[] = [...]` near the top. The `.some((c) => str.toLowerCase() === c.toLowerCase())` logic and contents stay byte-identical. Pure constant hoist (allowed by scraper rule). Medium: scraper file.

title: Remove dead url field from the ICA FilmInfo interface
detail: FilmInfo (lines 12-19) declares `url: string`, set only to `""` (line 121) and never read anywhere (verified zero reads). Remove `url: string` from the interface and `url: ""` from the literal → `const info: FilmInfo = { title };`. The booking URL actually used comes from bookingBase/fallbackUrl (lines 156-170). Leave `country`/`runtime` alone — removing them would mean deleting regex-extraction blocks (scraper rule forbids); only the constant-"" url field is in scope. Medium: scraper file.

title: Hoist per-filmId Array.filter out of classifyScope tmdb_mismatch loop
detail: In the tmdb_mismatch branch, `tmdbMismatches.filter((i) => i.entityId === filmId)` runs inside the `for (const filmId of filmIds)` loop — O(n^2) over the same array. Pre-group once into a Map<entityId, ClassifiedIssue[]> before the loop, then look up by filmId. Each matched issue still gets scope='systemic' under the identical futureScreenings.length>0 condition. Medium: gates DB writes downstream; grouping must preserve the exact same set/order of mutated issues.

title: Parallelize 4 independent DB reads in getUserProductData
detail: getUserProductData() awaits 4 fully independent Drizzle queries sequentially (statusCounts groupBy, firstInteraction asc-limit-1, lastInteraction desc-limit-1, preferences select-limit-1). Wrap in a single Promise.all([...]) and destructure. Same queries/rows/downstream computation; latency drops sum→max. Keep the surrounding try/catch so a rejection still returns null. Runs per-user in syncAllUsersToPostHog's loop so it compounds. Medium: touches DB execution (no result change).

title: Avoid calling buildConfig() twice per entry during wave ranking
detail: In runWave's ranking .map, entryScreeningCount (line 251) and entryStaleness (line 235) each call getEntryCinemaIds(entry) → entry.buildConfig() (line 226), so buildConfig runs twice per entry per wave (some closures instantiate scraper objects, e.g. createCurzonScraper). Compute `const ids = getEntryCinemaIds(entry)` once in the .map, change both helpers to accept `ids: string[]`, pass ids to each. cinema-id list is deterministic so sort keys/ordering unchanged. Medium: scrape orchestrator ranking path — reviewer should confirm buildConfig has no side effects before merging.
